task_completion.description: Apply 'Heading 1' and 'Heading 2' styles to the document's sections and subsections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for headings like 'Getting Started with Git' or 'Installing Git' to be formatted with larger, bold text typical of Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles. The styles dropdown in the toolbar may show 'Heading 1' or 'Heading 2' when a heading is selected.

task_completion.description: Format NOTE callouts by removing the marker, adding a left border, and applying a light blue background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for paragraphs containing text like 'Git stores data as a series of snapshots'. The '[NOTE]' marker should be removed, and the paragraph should have a visible colored left border and a light blue background shading.

task_completion.description: Format WARNING callouts by removing the marker, adding a left border, and applying a light red or yellow background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for paragraphs containing text like 'If you lose your local .git directory'. The '[WARNING]' marker should be removed, and the paragraph should have a visible colored left border and a light red or yellow background shading.

task_completion.description: Format TIP callouts by removing the marker, adding a left border, and applying a light green background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for paragraphs containing text like 'You can use git config --global alias'. The '[TIP]' marker should be removed, and the paragraph should have a visible colored left border and a light green background shading.

task_completion.description: Format CODE callouts by removing the marker, applying a monospace font, and adding a light gray background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for paragraphs containing text like 'git config --global user.name'. The '[CODE]' marker should be removed, and the text should be formatted in a monospace font with a light gray background shading.

task_completion.description: Save the final styled document as git_guide_styled.docx.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the LibreOffice Writer window title bar to display 'git_guide_styled.docx' or a 'Save As' dialog confirming the file is being saved with this exact name in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used LibreOffice Writer to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent is using LibreOffice Writer (the word processor GUI) to perform the task, not a plain text editor or a terminal script.

integrity.description: The agent used actual formatting tools rather than drawing shapes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent used the application's built-in formatting tools (e.g., Paragraph properties, Borders, Area fill, Font settings) rather than drawing colored rectangles or lines over the text.

integrity.description: The main text content of the document remains intact.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the actual text of the guide (other than the removed markers) was not deleted, altered, or replaced with dummy text.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Target Headings:
Heading 1: Getting Started with Git, Working with Repositories, Branching and Merging, Best Practices.
Heading 2: Installing Git, Initial Configuration, Remote Repositories.

Target Callout Texts (to identify paragraphs):
NOTE: 'Git stores data as a series of snapshots', 'The staging area is a file', 'Git does not explicitly track file movement', 'You can check the status of your files'.
WARNING: 'If you lose your local .git directory', 'Changing the commit history', 'Hard resetting will discard'.
TIP: 'You can use git config --global alias', 'Use git diff --cached', 'Pressing Tab will auto-complete'.
CODE: 'git config --global user.name', 'git clone https://github.com', 'git checkout -b feature-branch', 'git merge feature-branch', 'git push origin main'.

Output file path: /home/ga/Documents/git_guide_styled.docx
